message:
feat(runtime): wire epoch-tracking identifiers into tracing observability

Activate previously-silenced identifiers across the epoch GC pipeline so
they flow into tracing-integration diagnostics. The underscore-prefixed
parameters were placeholders left over from an earlier iteration where
cleanup bodies had no observers; now that the tracing hooks reference
them (waker_id, region_id, task_id, timer_id, channel_id, obligation_id,
correlation_id, violation_id, etc.), we drop the leading underscore so
rustc accepts them as used.

Changes:

- src/types/id.rs: add TaskId::as_u64() returning a packed
  (generation<<32) | index 64-bit representation. This is the canonical
  serializer for structured-log fields and bead-trace correlation, and
  replaces several ad-hoc expansions scattered through the GC paths.

- src/runtime/epoch_gc.rs: rename underscored parameters to their live
  counterparts in DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_obligation /
  cleanup_waker / cleanup_region / cleanup_timer / cleanup_channel so
  the tracing::debug! / tracing::trace! sites actually see the ids they
  log. Uses the new TaskId::as_u64() helper in the per-task trace line
  emitted during region cleanup.

- src/runtime/epoch_gc_integration.rs: mirror the same rename across
  direct_cleanup_obligation / direct_cleanup_waker /
  direct_cleanup_region / direct_cleanup_timer /
  direct_cleanup_channel so the integration-layer debug logs carry the
  ids they're already formatting into their message templates.

- src/runtime/epoch_tracker.rs: activate correlation_id, sync_status,
  processing_latency, violation_id, affected_modules, epoch_skew, and
  several destructured fields of EpochConsistencyViolation variants
  (ModuleDesync, SlowTransition, MissingTransition,
  AdvancementOrderViolation) so the structured error! records include
  the causal metadata they were designed to carry.

No behavioral change when tracing-integration is disabled; all new
field usage is gated by cfg(feature = "tracing-integration") or feeds
existing unconditional tracing calls.
